title: Hopscotch results.json
description: Public, versioned structured output of a hopscotch run. Written to `<projectDir>/.lake/hopscotch/results.json` after every state transition, and optionally mirrored to the path given by `--results-json PATH`.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version for this file. Bumped on any backward-incompatible change; independent of the runner's internal state.json schema.

status.description: Coarse run phase. `running` = a probe is in progress or queued. `failed` = the search found a failing commit (or a git-cleanliness check blocked resume). `completed` = every commit passed (linear mode, or bisect with --keep-last-good and an all-good range).

exitCode.description: Exit code the CLI would return for this state: 1 when `status = failed`, 0 otherwise. (The `2` tool-error code is never written here — a tool error aborts before a state transition.)

strategyScope.description: Identity of the bump/verify strategy. For `hopscotch dep <name>` this is the dependency name; for `hopscotch toolchain` it is `toolchain`.

projectDir.description: Absolute, realpath-normalized path to the downstream project root.

items.description: The full ordered list of probed items (commit SHAs in `dep` mode; toolchain strings in `toolchain` mode).

firstFailingCommit.description: The first failing commit when `status = failed`; `null` otherwise. In bisect mode this equals `items[bisect.knownBadIndex]`.

lastSuccessfulCommit.description: The most recent commit observed to succeed. `null` if no commit has passed yet (e.g. the very first commit failed). In bisect mode, equals `items[bisect.knownGoodIndex]` once any probe has succeeded.

currentCommit.description: The commit the run is currently positioned at. While `status = running` this is the next commit to probe; when `failed`, the failing commit; when `completed`, typically `null`.

failureStage.description: Which step of the probe failed, when `status = failed`. `lake update` = the bump step (rewrite lakefile/toolchain + `lake update`). `lake build` = a verify step. `git cleanliness check` = resume was blocked because the working tree is dirty after a fix (linear mode only).

nextIndex.description: Index into `items` the runner is currently positioned at. After a completed linear run this equals `items.length`. In bisect failed state it equals `bisect.knownBadIndex`.

culpritLogPath.description: Absolute path to the failing log inside the culprit subdirectory (`.lake/hopscotch/logs/culprit/`). Populated iff `status = failed` and a log was written. The file has the same basename as `lastLogPath` — both point at the same content, but the `culprit/` copy is durable across resumes.

lastLogPath.description: Absolute path to the log file from the most recent probe step (successful or failing). Naming: `{logPrefix}-{shortSha}-{stage}.log`. See CLAUDE.md for the log prefix scheme.

logsDir.description: Absolute path to the logs directory (`.lake/hopscotch/logs/`).

summaryPath.description: Absolute path to the companion human-readable `summary.md`.

updatedAt.description: UTC timestamp of the last state transition, ISO-8601 (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`). Mirrors `PersistedState.updatedAt`.

knownGoodIndex.description: Index of the most recent known-good commit. Starts at 0 (range lower bound, assumed good) and only increases.

knownBadIndex.description: Index of the known-bad endpoint. Starts at `items.length - 1` (range upper bound) and only decreases. When `status = failed` the window is adjacent: `knownBadIndex == knownGoodIndex + 1`.

knownGoodWasProbed.description: `true` iff the commit at `knownGoodIndex` has actually been probed and succeeded. `false` when the good endpoint was only assumed (the typical case for `items[0]` at the start of a run).

verifiedBad.description: `true` once the initial bad endpoint has been probed and confirmed to fail. The binary-search loop does not start probing midpoints until this is true.

probeResults.description: Cached results of every commit probed so far. Ordered by probe completion time (most recent last). Consumers can reconstruct the bisect path taken from this array.

commit.description: The probed item (commit SHA or toolchain string).

outcome.description: Whether the probe passed or failed.

logPath.description: Absolute path to the log from this probe. Populated on failure; may be `null` on success.
